reasoning: The 10-year real interest rate dropped sharply to 1.67 in April 2025, which is notably below the 1-year trailing average of ~1.85. This below-trend reading implies a greater likelihood of an increase (mean reversion) in the coming months. The linear model from the FRED forecast estimates a 61% probability of an increase by May. Following the explicit prompt instructions that probabilities constraint for distant dates should be closer to 0.5 (indicating higher uncertainty), the estimates for July and October are tapered toward 50%.
